title: erigeron lassenianus
intro: Erigeron lassenianus, also known as Lassen's fleabane or Lassen's daisy, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to California in the United States. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Erigeron lassenianus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. Erigeron lassenianus can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or oil, and then applying the resulting liquid or paste topically or internally.
